refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-003 Refined: The NGHE solution shall enable operators and tele-operators to achieve a measured energy efficiency of energy_per_ton ≤ E_target (customer-defined threshold) over the agreed evaluation period, through integrated monitoring, control optimization, and real-time feedback mechanisms.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is outcome-focused but lacks explicit linkage to the mechanisms (monitoring, control, feedback) that enable efficiency achievement. Refinement clarifies that energy efficiency is not a passive property but an active capability requiring measurement, visibility, and operator decision support. This aligns with stakeholder needs (Site Manager tracking KPI, Operator optimizing work patterns, Safety Manager ensuring safe efficiency practices).
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure energy_per_ton = total_energy_consumed / total_tonnage_moved over a defined evaluation period (e.g., 1 week, 1 month) in representative worksite conditions. Compare against E_target. Verify that measurement system is calibrated and that feedback mechanisms are accessible to operators.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log energy consumption (kWh or equivalent) and work output (tonnage moved) with ≥95% data availability.
CR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time energy efficiency feedback (energy_per_ton metric) to operators and tele-operators via telemetry interface.

CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall support operator control strategies (e.g., load optimization, cycle time reduction, idle minimization) that demonstrably reduce energy_per_ton without compromising safety.
CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE solution shall log and report energy efficiency performance data to Site Manager for KPI tracking and continuous improvement.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Implement energy monitoring subsystem (power sensors, data logging) + operator feedback dashboard (real-time energy_per_ton display) + control advisory system (recommend load/cycle optimizations) integrated across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

DP-B: Embed energy efficiency as a constraint in autonomous vehicle control logic; minimize energy_per_ton through algorithmic optimization (route planning, load balancing, idle reduction) with operator override capability.

DP-C: Centralize energy efficiency management in a dedicated Energy Optimization Service (separate from Vehicle and Teleoperation); expose energy_per_ton metric via Telemetry interface; allow Site Manager to set E_target and receive performance alerts.

DP-D: Hybrid approach: Vehicle provides raw energy/work data via Telemetry; Teleoperation module processes data and displays feedback to operator; Safety Services validates that efficiency strategies do not violate safety constraints; NGHE Solution orchestrates data flow and KPI reporting.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Hybrid approach with distributed responsibility: Vehicle module measures and reports energy consumption and work output via Telemetry interface. Teleoperation module processes telemetry data, computes energy_per_ton in real-time, and displays feedback to operator/tele-operator. Safety Services validates that operator-selected efficiency strategies (load optimization, cycle adjustments) comply with worksite safety plan. NGHE Solution (top-level orchestrator) aggregates performance data, compares against E_target, and generates KPI reports for Site Manager. This design distributes concerns appropriately: Vehicle owns measurement, Teleoperation owns operator feedback, Safety Services owns safety validation, NGHE Solution owns system-level KPI tracking.

potential_sources: Telemetry interface must carry both energy and work-output data; design must ensure Vehicle and Teleoperation agree on data semantics (units, sampling rate, latency tolerance).

Safety Services validation of efficiency strategies could create feedback loop: if a strategy is deemed unsafe, operator must be notified and alternative recommended. This requires bidirectional communication between Teleoperation and Safety Services.
NGHE Solution KPI reporting depends on aggregated data from Vehicle (via Teleoperation) and Safety Services (compliance status). Timing and consistency of data aggregation must be specified.
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is manageable via well-defined interfaces (Telemetry, SafetyAlert). The design avoids tight coupling by using asynchronous data flows and clear ownership boundaries. Risk: if Telemetry interface is underspecified, Vehicle and Teleoperation may interpret energy data differently, leading to incorrect KPI calculations.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Vehicle has onboard power measurement capability (e.g., fuel flow sensor, battery current/voltage monitoring) with ≥95% availability.

ASSUMPTION: Teleoperation module has sufficient computational capacity to process real-time telemetry and compute energy_per_ton without introducing unacceptable latency (target: <5 sec feedback delay).
ASSUMPTION: Safety Services can evaluate efficiency strategies against worksite safety plan in near-real-time (target: <10 sec decision latency).
RISK: If Vehicle measurement is inaccurate or drifts over time, KPI calculations will be unreliable. Mitigation: periodic calibration and cross-validation with external energy meters.
RISK: Operator may ignore or misinterpret energy efficiency feedback if not presented clearly. Mitigation: user experience testing and iterative refinement of feedback UI.

RISK: Safety Services validation could become a bottleneck if efficiency strategies are complex. Mitigation: pre-approve common strategies (e.g., standard load ranges) and only validate novel strategies in real-time.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the Telemetry interface specification: define exact data fields (energy_consumed, work_output, timestamp, quality_flags), sampling rate (e.g., 1 Hz), and latency tolerance. Define the SafetyAlert interface for bidirectional communication between Teleoperation and Safety Services (e.g., 'proposed_strategy' -> 'safety_verdict' + 'reason'). Specify KPI aggregation logic in NGHE Solution: how are multiple Vehicle instances (if fleet) aggregated? How is E_target comparison performed (rolling window, daily, weekly)?

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: System-level orchestrator responsible for energy efficiency KPI management: receives energy and work-output telemetry from Vehicle (via Teleoperation), receives safety validation status from Safety Services, aggregates performance data, compares energy_per_ton against E_target, generates KPI reports for Site Manager, and triggers alerts if E_target is at risk.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives energy consumption (kWh), work output (tonnage), timestamp, and data quality flags from Vehicle via Teleoperation module. Used for real-time KPI calculation and historical trend analysis.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends KPI status, E_target comparison results, and efficiency recommendations to Site Manager. May also send control directives to Teleoperation if autonomous efficiency optimization is enabled.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety validation status from Safety Services regarding proposed efficiency strategies. Used to ensure KPI tracking does not compromise safety compliance.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Provides connectivity for NGHE Solution to communicate with Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Site Manager. Critical for real-time KPI updates and alert dissemination.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural system-level owner of KPI management and reporting. It orchestrates data from Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. High containment because KPI aggregation, E_target comparison, and Site Manager reporting are system-level concerns. Low diffusion because logic is centralized in one module. Minor diffusion: depends on Telemetry and SafetyAlert interfaces being well-defined.

Vehicle owns energy measurement and work-output sensing, which are core to the FR. However, KPI calculation, feedback to operator, and Site Manager reporting are not Vehicle responsibilities. If KPI logic were embedded in Vehicle, it would create tight coupling with Teleoperation and Safety Services. Moderate containment because measurement is essential but not sufficient for the full FR.

Teleoperation owns operator feedback and real-time energy_per_ton display, which are critical for operator decision-making. However, KPI aggregation, E_target comparison, and Site Manager reporting belong at system level. If Teleoperation owned all KPI logic, it would blur the boundary between operator-facing feedback and system-level reporting. Moderate containment because feedback is essential but not sufficient.

Safety Services validates that efficiency strategies comply with safety constraints. This is a necessary but not sufficient part of the FR. Safety Services should not own KPI calculation or Site Manager reporting. Moderate-low containment because safety validation is a constraint on efficiency, not the primary mechanism.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the optimal owner because: (1) KPI management (energy_per_ton calculation, E_target comparison, reporting) is a system-level concern that requires data from multiple modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services); (2) Site Manager is a system-level stakeholder who needs system-level KPI visibility; (3) centralizing KPI logic in NGHE Solution avoids duplication and ensures consistency; (4) NGHE Solution can orchestrate data flows and trigger alerts without tight coupling to individual modules.
containment_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution KPI logic is too tightly coupled to specific Vehicle or Teleoperation implementations, it may not generalize to different vehicle types or tele-operation modes. MITIGATION: Define Telemetry interface as a contract; ensure NGHE Solution depends only on the interface, not on implementation details.

RISK: NGHE Solution may become a bottleneck if KPI calculation is computationally expensive or if data aggregation latency is high. MITIGATION: Implement KPI calculation in a scalable manner (e.g., streaming aggregation, caching); specify acceptable latency (e.g., <10 sec for KPI updates).

RISK: If Safety Services validation is slow, NGHE Solution may not be able to provide timely feedback to operators. MITIGATION: Pre-approve common efficiency strategies; only validate novel strategies in real-time; use SafetyAlert interface to communicate validation status asynchronously.

RISK: NGHE Solution may lack visibility into Vehicle-level energy measurement quality (e.g., sensor drift, calibration status). MITIGATION: Vehicle should include data quality flags in Telemetry; NGHE Solution should monitor and alert on quality degradation.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Specify the NGHE Solution module boundary more precisely: (1) Define the KPI calculation algorithm (e.g., rolling average, exponential smoothing) and E_target comparison logic. (2) Specify the data aggregation strategy for multi-vehicle scenarios (if applicable). (3) Define the alert thresholds and notification mechanisms for Site Manager. (4) Clarify the relationship between NGHE Solution and Teleoperation: does NGHE Solution send efficiency recommendations back to Teleoperation, or only to Site Manager? (5) Ensure that NGHE Solution does not duplicate energy measurement or operator feedback logic; it should orchestrate, not replicate.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-D (hybrid distributed approach) directly addresses the refined FR. The DP specifies how each module contributes to energy efficiency: Vehicle measures, Teleoperation provides feedback, Safety Services validates, NGHE Solution aggregates and reports. The DP is traceable to all sub-FRs (measurement, feedback, control support, reporting). No independence violations detected; each module has a distinct responsibility.

diffusion_risks: Telemetry interface must be precisely specified to avoid misinterpretation of energy/work-output data between Vehicle and Teleoperation/NGHE Solution.
SafetyAlert interface must support bidirectional communication (strategy proposal -> validation verdict) to enable real-time safety validation without blocking operator feedback.
Network Link must provide sufficient bandwidth and latency for real-time KPI updates (target: <5 sec feedback to operator, <10 sec KPI update to Site Manager).
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the appropriate owner of system-level KPI management. Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services are appropriately scoped to their core responsibilities (measurement, feedback, safety validation). No significant diffusion of KPI logic across modules. Risk: if Telemetry or SafetyAlert interfaces are underspecified, modules may implement incompatible interpretations, leading to KPI calculation errors.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible: (1) Measure energy_per_ton in controlled worksite conditions and compare against E_target. (2) Verify that Telemetry interface carries accurate energy and work-output data. (3) Verify that operator feedback is displayed within acceptable latency. (4) Verify that Safety Services validation does not prevent safe efficiency strategies. (5) Verify that Site Manager receives accurate KPI reports. All verification activities are testable and measurable.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: Vehicle owns measurement, Teleoperation owns operator feedback, Safety Services owns safety validation, NGHE Solution owns KPI aggregation and reporting. Each module has a distinct, non-overlapping responsibility. Interfaces (Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Tasking, Network Link) are clearly defined as boundaries between modules.

recommended_revisions: Refine the Telemetry interface specification: define exact data fields, units, sampling rate, latency tolerance, and quality flags. Example: {energy_consumed_kWh: float, work_output_tons: float, timestamp_ms: int, quality_flag: enum(GOOD|DEGRADED|POOR)}.

Refine the SafetyAlert interface specification: define the format for efficiency strategy proposals and safety validation verdicts. Example: {strategy_id: string, strategy_description: string, proposed_by: enum(OPERATOR|NGHE_SOLUTION), safety_verdict: enum(APPROVED|REJECTED|CONDITIONAL), reason: string}.
Specify the KPI calculation algorithm in NGHE Solution: rolling average window (e.g., 1 hour, 1 day), handling of missing data, outlier detection, and E_target comparison logic.
Define the alert thresholds and notification mechanisms: when should Site Manager be alerted if E_target is at risk? (e.g., if energy_per_ton exceeds 90% of E_target for 2 consecutive hours).
Clarify the role of Tasking interface: does NGHE Solution send efficiency recommendations back to Teleoperation/Vehicle, or only status/alerts to Site Manager?

decision.justification: The refined FR is decomposable into four sub-FRs (measurement, feedback, control support, reporting), each with distinct ownership and verification criteria. The selected DP (hybrid distributed approach) is sound and traceable to the FR. The assigned IM (NGHE Solution as orchestrator) is appropriate for system-level KPI management. However, the sub-FRs require further decomposition to specify detailed requirements for each module (Vehicle measurement subsystem, Teleoperation feedback UI, Safety Services validation logic, NGHE Solution KPI engine). Additionally, interface specifications (Telemetry, SafetyAlert) must be refined before detailed design can proceed. Proceeding to the next decomposition level is necessary to ensure that all sub-FRs are adequately addressed and that interface contracts are well-defined.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-003.1: The NGHE solution shall continuously measure and log energy consumption (kWh or equivalent) and work output (tonnage moved) with ≥95% data availability. [OWNER: Vehicle module]
CR-NGHE-003.2: The NGHE solution shall provide real-time energy efficiency feedback (energy_per_ton metric) to operators and tele-operators via telemetry interface. [OWNER: Teleoperation module]

CR-NGHE-003.3: The NGHE solution shall support operator control strategies (e.g., load optimization, cycle time reduction, idle minimization) that demonstrably reduce energy_per_ton without compromising safety. [OWNER: Teleoperation + Safety Services]
CR-NGHE-003.4: The NGHE solution shall log and report energy efficiency performance data to Site Manager for KPI tracking and continuous improvement. [OWNER: NGHE Solution]

assumptions: Vehicle has onboard power measurement capability (fuel flow, battery current/voltage) with ≥95% availability and <5% measurement error.
Teleoperation module has sufficient computational capacity to process real-time telemetry and compute energy_per_ton with <5 sec latency.
Safety Services can evaluate efficiency strategies against worksite safety plan with <10 sec decision latency.
Site Manager has access to a dashboard or reporting system to view KPI data and receive alerts.
Network Link provides sufficient bandwidth (>100 kbps) and latency (<500 ms) for real-time KPI updates.
E_target is defined by customer and remains stable over the evaluation period (or changes are communicated to NGHE Solution).
Worksite safety plan is documented and accessible to Safety Services for validation of efficiency strategies.
open_risks: Vehicle energy measurement accuracy and drift over time; mitigation: periodic calibration and cross-validation.
Teleoperation feedback latency may exceed acceptable threshold if network is congested; mitigation: implement priority queuing for KPI updates.
Safety Services validation may become a bottleneck if efficiency strategies are complex; mitigation: pre-approve common strategies.
Operator may ignore or misinterpret energy efficiency feedback; mitigation: user experience testing and iterative refinement.
NGHE Solution KPI calculation may not generalize to different vehicle types or work environments; mitigation: design for modularity and extensibility.
Telemetry interface may be underspecified, leading to incompatible implementations; mitigation: define interface contract early and validate with all stakeholders.
evidence_needed: Vehicle energy measurement specification: sensor type, accuracy, sampling rate, calibration procedure.
Telemetry interface specification: data fields, units, sampling rate, latency tolerance, quality flags.
SafetyAlert interface specification: strategy proposal format, validation verdict format, decision latency SLA.
Teleoperation feedback UI mockup: energy_per_ton display, operator recommendations, alert presentation.
Safety Services validation logic: rules for evaluating efficiency strategies against worksite safety plan.
NGHE Solution KPI engine specification: calculation algorithm, E_target comparison logic, alert thresholds, reporting format.
Network Link capacity analysis: bandwidth and latency requirements for real-time KPI updates.
User acceptance testing results: operator feedback on energy efficiency feedback clarity and usefulness.
Field trial data: energy_per_ton measurements in representative worksite conditions, comparison against E_target.
